summary: This is a brief pointer post directing readers to a Bloomberg column O'Neil wrote about COVID-19 epidemiological models. The column, informed by mathematician Jarod Alper's YouTube talk, examines how various COVID models work and their limitations. O'Neil signals her critical stance through the subtitle indicating that while no models are perfect, some fail more fundamentally than others.
key_points: O'Neil wrote a Bloomberg column analyzing COVID-19 prediction models
The analysis draws on mathematician Jarod Alper's explanatory YouTube talk
The framing suggests a spectrum of model quality, from imperfect to fundamentally flawed
money_quotes: None are perfect; some are downright dumb.
key_insight: COVID-19 models vary dramatically in quality, and understanding their limitations is essential for interpreting pandemic predictions.
